question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user suppress gossip of a transaction by exploiting the fact that Downloader.verifySignatures() returns false (without calling onVerified()) when all CONSENSUS_REACHED signatures fail data file verification, and the error is only logged, causing the transaction to be silently dropped with no retry mechanism?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user prevent gossip of transactions by exploiting the @Order(Ordered.HIGHEST_PRECEDENCE) annotation on BatchStreamFileNotifier, where if this notifier is injected first in CompositeStreamFileNotifier's list and throws an exception, all other notifiers (including any audit or monitoring notifiers) are skipped, creating a blind spot in transaction tracking?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user suppress gossip of a transaction by exploiting the fact that BatchStreamFileNotifier.handle() only calls reset() after a successful flush, so if the buffer grows to contain a target transaction and then a subsequent parse failure occurs, the buffer is never cleared and the transaction is re-parsed repeatedly but never forwarded to downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user prevent gossip of multiple transactions by exploiting the fact that the ArrayBlockingQueue in BatchStreamFileNotifier has a fixed queueCapacity, and by submitting a large number of crafted StreamFiles that are slow to parse, the queue fills up and queue.put() blocks the calling thread indefinitely, creating a denial-of-service on the entire verified() dispatch path?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user suppress gossip of a transaction by exploiting the fact that Downloader.verifyHashChain() returns true when streamFile.getType().isChained() is false, allowing a crafted non-chained StreamFile to bypass hash chain verification entirely and potentially replace a legitimate file in the notification pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user prevent gossip of transactions by exploiting the fact that BatchStreamFileNotifier.flush() uses items.addAndGet() which is not reset atomically with files.incrementAndGet(), creating a TOCTOU window where concurrent verified() calls from multiple threads could cause the flush decision to be based on stale counter values?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user suppress gossip of a transaction by exploiting the fact that BlockStreamVerifier.verify() re-throws exceptions after setting success=false, causing the streamVerificationMeterProvider to record a failure but the streamFileNotifier.verified() call to be skipped, permanently preventing that block's transactions from being notified?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user prevent gossip of multiple transactions by exploiting the fact that CompositeStreamFileNotifier.verified() has no atomicity guarantee across its notifier list, so if the system crashes or is interrupted mid-iteration, some notifiers receive the verified event and others do not, creating an inconsistent state where transactions are partially gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user suppress gossip of a transaction by crafting a StreamFile with getMetadataHash() that does not match the signature's getMetadataHashAsHex(), causing verifyHash() to throw HashMismatchException after the file hash passes, preventing onVerified() from being called and silencing that file's transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user prevent gossip of transactions by exploiting the fact that BatchStreamFileNotifier.StreamFileSubscriber.buffer is an unsynchronized ArrayList, and if the executorService creates additional threads that call handle() concurrently, a ConcurrentModificationException during buffer iteration in streamFileParser.parse(new ArrayList<>(buffer)) could cause transactions to be dropped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user suppress gossip of a transaction by exploiting the fact that Downloader.onVerified() calls streamFile.copy().clear() to cache a cleared copy in lastStreamFile, but if copy() or clear() throws an exception, lastStreamFile is not updated and the downloader will re-process the same file, potentially causing duplicate or skipped transaction notifications?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user prevent gossip of multiple transactions by exploiting the fact that BatchStreamFileNotifier uses Executors.newFixedThreadPool(2) with no rejection handler, so if both threads are blocked and the queue is full, any new verified() call will block indefinitely in queue.put(), creating a system-wide stall that prevents all transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user suppress gossip of a transaction by exploiting the fact that Downloader.verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles() skips files with SignatureVerificationException when sigFilenameIter.hasNext() is true, causing the target file to be silently skipped in favor of the next file, permanently dropping its transactions from the notification pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user prevent gossip of transactions by exploiting the fact that BatchStreamFileNotifier.flush() checks exceedsInterval() as a fallback, but if lastFlush is set to Instant.now() at construction time and the flushInterval is very large, transactions can be buffered for an arbitrarily long time before being forwarded to the parser?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user suppress gossip of a transaction by exploiting the fact that BlockStreamVerifier.verify() calls blockFileTransformer.transform(blockFile) before streamFileNotifier.verified(), so if transform() throws an exception, verified() is never called and the block's transactions are permanently dropped from the gossip pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user prevent gossip of multiple transactions by exploiting the fact that the queue.poll(frequency, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS) in BatchStreamFileNotifier.StreamFileSubscriber.run() uses the parser's frequency as a timeout, so if frequency is set to a very large value, the subscriber thread sleeps for a long time between polls, causing significant delays in transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user suppress gossip of a transaction by exploiting the fact that Downloader.verifySignatures() only processes signatures with status CONSENSUS_REACHED, so if an attacker can cause all signatures for a target file to have a different status (e.g., VERIFIED but not CONSENSUS_REACHED), onVerified() is never called and the file's transactions are silently dropped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user prevent gossip of transactions by exploiting the fact that BatchStreamFileNotifier.handle() adds the streamFile to the buffer before checking if flush() returns true, so if flush() throws an unchecked exception (e.g., due to AtomicLong overflow), the file is added to the buffer but reset() is never called, causing the buffer to grow indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user suppress gossip of a transaction by exploiting the fact that CutoverServiceImpl.verified() only updates lastRecordFile when the streamFile is an instance of RecordFile, so if a non-RecordFile StreamFile is passed to verified(), the lastRecordFile state is not updated and subsequent block number verification may fail, causing future transactions to be dropped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user prevent gossip of multiple transactions by exploiting the fact that BatchStreamFileNotifier.close() calls executorService.close() which waits for all tasks to complete, and if a subscriber thread is blocked in queue.put() or parse(), the close() call blocks indefinitely, preventing the system from restarting and resuming transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user suppress gossip of a transaction by exploiting the fact that Downloader.setStreamFileIndex() reads from lastStreamFile.get() without synchronization, so a concurrent update to lastStreamFile between the read and the setIndex() call could cause the index to be set incorrectly, causing downstream consumers to reject the file as out-of-sequence?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user prevent gossip of transactions by exploiting the fact that BatchStreamFileNotifier.flush() calls items.addAndGet(streamFile.getCount()) before checking maxFiles, so if getCount() returns a very large value that causes items to exceed maxItems, the flush is triggered but the items counter is not reset until reset() is called, potentially causing double-flush of the same transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user suppress gossip of a transaction by exploiting the fact that Downloader.downloadAndParseSigFiles() uses onErrorContinue() to silently skip nodes that fail signature download, so if an attacker can cause signature download failures for all nodes holding a target transaction's file, no signatures are collected and the file is never verified or notified?
